fever.name: Fever (बुखार)
fever.description: Body temperature above 98.6°F (37°C). Common symptom of infection.
what_to_do: Rest and drink plenty of fluids (water, ORS, coconut water)
Take paracetamol (500mg for adults, dose by weight for children)
Use wet cloth on forehead for comfort
Monitor temperature every 4-6 hours
Wear light clothing, don't over-bundle
when_to_see_doctor: Fever with severe headache and stiff neck

diarrhea.name: Diarrhea (दस्त/उल्टी)
diarrhea.description: Loose, watery stools occurring 3+ times per day. Can cause dehydration quickly.
what_to_do: ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) — most important! Mix 1 sachet in 1 litre clean water
Continue breastfeeding for infants
Drink boiled and cooled water
Eat light food — rice, banana, toast, curd
Avoid spicy, oily, and milk-based food
Zinc tablet (20mg for children) for 10-14 days helps
when_to_see_doctor: Signs of dehydration (dry mouth, no tears, sunken eyes, less urine)
diarrhea.ors_recipe: 6 level teaspoons sugar + 1/2 level teaspoon salt in 1 litre clean boiled water

cough and cold.name: Cough & Cold (खांसी-जुकाम)
cough and cold.description: Common viral infection. Usually resolves in 7-10 days.
what_to_do: Rest and stay warm
Drink warm water, tea with ginger and honey
Steam inhalation (cover head with towel over hot water)
Gargle with warm salt water for sore throat
Honey (1-2 teaspoons) for cough — NOT for children under 1 year
Keep room ventilated
when_to_see_doctor: High fever (>101°F) for more than 3 days

headache.name: Headache (सिरदर्द)
headache.description: Pain in any region of the head. Usually not serious, but can indicate underlying issues.
what_to_do: Rest in a quiet, dark room
Apply cold or warm compress to forehead
Drink water — dehydration is a common cause
Take paracetamol (500mg) or ibuprofen (400mg)
Massage temples gently
Check if you've eaten — low blood sugar can cause headache
when_to_see_doctor: Sudden severe headache ('worst headache of your life')
Headache with fever and stiff neck
Headache with weakness or numbness on one side
Daily headaches for more than 2 weeks

stomach pain.name: Stomach Pain (पेट दर्द)
stomach pain.description: Pain or discomfort in the abdomen. Can have many causes.
what_to_do: Rest and avoid heavy food
Drink clear fluids — water, clear soup
Apply warm compress to abdomen
Avoid spicy, oily, and gas-producing food
Antacid (for acidity): Gelusil, Digene
when_to_see_doctor: Severe pain, can't stand up straight
Pain in right lower abdomen (appendix risk)

chest pain.name: Chest Pain (छाती में दर्द)
chest pain.description: ⚠️ Can be serious. If related to heart, every minute counts.
what_to_do: ⚠️ If pain is crushing, radiating to arm/jaw, with sweating — CALL 108 IMMEDIATELY
Stop all activity, sit or lie down
Chew one aspirin (325mg) if available and not allergic
Loosen tight clothing
when_to_see_doctor: ⚠️ ANY chest pain should be evaluated by a doctor
Pain spreading to arm, jaw, neck, or back
Nausea, dizziness, sweating with chest pain
chest pain.emergency: Call 108 (ambulance) or 102 immediately. Time is critical for heart attacks.

skin rash.name: Skin Rash / Allergy (त्वचा पर दाने)
skin rash.description: Red, itchy, or irritated skin. Can be due to allergy, infection, or irritation.
what_to_do: Keep the area clean and dry
Avoid scratching
Apply calamine lotion for itching
Wear loose, cotton clothing
Avoid known allergens (new soap, food, etc.)
Cetirizine tablet for allergic rashes

burns.name: Burns (जलना)
burns.description: Skin damage from heat, chemicals, or electricity.
what_to_do: Cool the burn under running cool water for 10-20 minutes immediately
Remove jewelry/clothing near burn before swelling
DO NOT apply ice, butter, toothpaste, or any paste
Cover with clean, non-stick bandage or clean cloth
Take paracetamol for pain
Keep burned area elevated if possible
when_to_see_doctor: Burn on face, hands, feet, groin, or joints
Burn in child or elderly person
Signs of infection (increased pain, swelling, pus)

breathing difficulty.name: Breathing Difficulty (सांस लेने में तकलीफ)
what_to_do: ⚠️ If severe — call 108 IMMEDIATELY
Sit upright, don't lie down
Loosen tight clothing
Open windows for fresh air
If known asthma, use inhaler
Stay calm — panic makes breathing harder
when_to_see_doctor: ⚠️ ALWAYS seek immediate medical help for breathing difficulty
breathing difficulty.emergency: Call 108 immediately. This can be a medical emergency.

malaria symptoms.name: Malaria (मलेरिया)
malaria symptoms.description: Mosquito-borne disease causing fever, chills, and flu-like symptoms.
what_to_do: Get blood test (Rapid Diagnostic Test or blood smear) immediately
Take prescribed anti-malarial drugs — complete full course
Rest and drink plenty of fluids
Use mosquito net even during day
Take paracetamol for fever
when_to_see_doctor: Fever with chills that come and go in cycles
prevention: Wear long sleeves in the evening

dengue symptoms.name: Dengue (डेंगू)
dengue symptoms.description: Mosquito-borne viral disease. Can be severe (dengue hemorrhagic fever).
what_to_do: Get blood test (NS1 antigen, CBC) immediately
Drink plenty of fluids — water, ORS, juice
Take paracetamol for fever — AVOID aspirin and ibuprofen
Monitor for warning signs daily
Rest, don't exert yourself
when_to_see_doctor: ⚠️ HIGH FEVER WITH SEVERE PAIN — seek immediate medical help
Severe joint and muscle pain ('breakbone fever')
Skin rash appearing 2-5 days after fever
prevention: Eliminate stagnant water (flower pots, tires, coolers)

typhoid.name: Typhoid (टायफाइड)
typhoid.description: Bacterial infection from contaminated food/water. Common in areas with poor sanitation.
what_to_do: Get blood test (Widal test or blood culture)
Complete full course of antibiotics as prescribed
Drink only boiled/purified water
Eat light, soft food — khichdi, dal, banana
Rest fully, don't rush back to work
when_to_see_doctor: Gradually increasing fever over several days
Stomach pain and constipation or diarrhea
prevention: Avoid street food with questionable hygiene
